target.name: Palantir
target.founder: Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Stephen Cohen, Joe Lonsdale, Nathan Gettings

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $400.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $2.4B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Liquid AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2.35B

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Palantir is a Series A-stage AI & Software company with a last post-money valuation of $400.0B and $3B+ in total pre-IPO capital raised. The relevant comp universe includes 1 public peer (PLTR) and 8 private comparables, with same-stage AI & Software companies including Liquid AI ($2.4B), Field AI ($2.0B), and Twelve Labs ($250M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $800.0B. Peer-median analysis across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $250M to $2.4B. The significant divergence between these two approaches reflects structural differences: stage-progression anchors to the company's exceptional last round, while peer-median reflects the current market for early-stage AI & Software financings.

Triangulation across both methods supports a range of $1.7B to $920.0B, with a median of $401.0B. The extreme width of this range reflects genuine uncertainty about whether Palantir's $400B+ last valuation represents sustainable market consensus or an outlier premium. Key uncertainties include the relevance of public-market comparables to a Series A private entity, the temporal gap between the $400B round and current market conditions, and the applicability of standard stage-progression multiples to a company of this scale and stage positioning.
